chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْحَجِّ مَاشِياً وَ انْقِطَاعِ مَشْيِ الْمَاشِي
chapter_title.en: Chapter 158 – The going to the Hajj walking and the cutting off the walk of the walker

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/110)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/110)
text: هُ سُئِلَ عَنِ الْحَجِّ مَاشِياً أَفْضَلُ أَوْ رَاكِباً قَالَ بَلْ رَاكِباً فَإِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) حَجَّ رَاكِباً .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Rafa’at, and Ibn Bukeyr,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having been asked about the Hajj walking being superior or riding?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘But, riding, for Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> went to Hajj riding’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Rifa‘ah and ibn Bukayr who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, was asked about traveling for al-Hajj if on foot was more virtuous or riding. He (the Imam) said, ‘Riding is more virtuous because the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, traveled for al-Hajj riding.’”
